title: Pungent Smoky Kenyan Lentil Stew
description: A smoky and creamy Kenyan dish featuring kombu, perfect for a monsoon day, this stew is egg-free-friendly and cooked in a traditional molcajete vessel.

ingredients.name: kombu
ingredients.notes: dried and cut into small pieces

instructions: Rinse the lentils and drain well, then set aside.
In a large molcajete, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, kombu pieces, and lentils, stir to combine.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer.
Add the kombu cold extract, coconut milk, salt, and black pepper, stir to combine.
Simmer the stew for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Remove the kombu pieces and serve hot.
